etymology.description: 木(木) (mù, "wood") + 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") -> referring to the tastiness of apricot fruit
etymology.interpretation: The combination of 木(木) (mù, "wood") and 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") intuitively suggests the appealing nature of apricots, as wood represents the tree and mouth denotes savoring the fruit.

examples.chinese: 我喜欢吃杏子(我喜歡吃杏子)。
杏树上结满了果实(杏樹上結滿了果實)。
examples.pinyin: Xìng shù shàng jié mǎnle guǒshí.
examples.english: The apricot tree is full of fruit.

examples.chinese: 春天时(春天時)，杏花开得特别美丽(杏花開得特別美麗)。
examples.pinyin: Chūntiān shí, xìnghuā kāi de tèbié měilì.
examples.english: In spring, apricot blossoms bloom beautifully.
